name: Field POP
description: The Field POP adds a Weight attribute that determines how much each point is within a 3D shape. Fields are bounding regions in 3D space. The shapes include spheres (ellipsoids), cylinders, planes, paraboloids, etc, where all points within the shape (or on one side of the shape) are given a Weight of 1 and all other points are 0. The Field POP is often used to isolate or cut out sections of points in point clouds. It also can output a Dist attribute, which is a distance to the surface for points that are outside the shape. The Transition Range parameter softens the Weight transition from 1 to 0. It is the distance over which the weight goes from 1 to 0. Transition Align determines where the transition from 1 to 0 starts. By default the weight is .5 at the Radius for a sphere field. if Align is -.5 the weight has dropped to 0 at the radius, and if align is .5, the weight is 1 at the radius and drops to 0 outside the radius. Similar for other field shapes. The Transition Type allows for linear, ease-in ease-out, or smooth step transitions from 1 to 0. '''Multiple fields in one Field POP''': A parameter called Specification POP is a pointer to a POP where each point is a specification defining a separate field - one point per field. The attributes of the specification POP are named the same as parameters tokens in the Field POP, and override the parameters for each field. Menus and toggles are represented as integers, starting at 0 for the first menu entry. You need only to create a Point POP with attributes named as the Field POP parameters, and then add a point per field, and set their values. (An attribute sizex will override the Size X (sizex) parameter.) Alternately you can generate points procedurally with attributes that match the Field POP parameter names. The workflow is the same as multiple strings defined in a specification DAT of the [[Geo Text COMP]] and the [[Text COMP]]. '''Using P as field position''': Although the rule is that the attribute name must be the same as the name of the parameter of the Field POP, P (position attribute) is a special case as it overrides tx, ty and tz parameters of the Field POP. If the node point1 has attributes radx and P, they override the Field POP's radx, tx, ty and tz parameters. '''Multiple weights raw or mixed''': When you specify multiple fields, their weights are mixed together into the Weight attribute (using a blend mode like Screen of the [[Composite TOP]] - combining multi-fields increase the Weight but it never exceeds 1). Alternately the weight for each field can be output raw in a multi-value Weights array attribute. The Weight or Distance attribute can be added/multiplied with any another attribute, like a PartForce attribute to confine forces to certain regions.

parameters.name: Attribute Class
parameters.label: Attribute Class
parameters.description: Makes the POP operate on point attributes, vertex attributes or primitive attributes where applicable.

parameters.name: Field Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Field Attribute Scope
parameters.description: Determines the scope of the attribute used to output the weight.

parameters.name: Specification POP
parameters.label: Specification POP
parameters.description: Reference to a POP where each point is a specification defining a separate field.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: Select the shape of the field.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.label: Size
parameters.description: The geometry 3D size.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description: Radius of field.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.label: Height
parameters.description: The height of the tube.

parameters.name: Roundness
parameters.label: Roundness
parameters.description: Roundness factor for rounded fields modes.

parameters.name: Point A
parameters.label: Point A
parameters.description: Shape first point.

parameters.name: Point B
parameters.label: Point B
parameters.description: Shape second point.

parameters.name: Strength
parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description: Sets the field strength.

parameters.name: Exponent
parameters.label: Exponent
parameters.description: Sets the exponent on parabola fields.

parameters.name: Transition Range
parameters.label: Transition Range
parameters.description: Determines a transition range for weights.

parameters.name: Transition Align
parameters.label: Transition Align
parameters.description: With Transition Range > 0, by default the weight is .5 at the Radius. if Align is -.5 the weight has dropped to 0 at the radius, and if align is .5, the weight is 1 at the radius and drops to 0 outside the radius.

parameters.name: Transition Type
parameters.label: Transition Type
parameters.description: Determines a transition function for weights.

parameters.name: Absolute Value
parameters.label: Absolute Value
parameters.description: Enable using the absolute value on the transitioned weight.

parameters.name: Invert
parameters.label: Invert
parameters.description: Invert the attribute value resulting from the field.

parameters.name: To Range
parameters.label: To Range
parameters.description: Sets the output range on the weigths.

parameters.name: Delete Zeros
parameters.label: Delete Zeros
parameters.description: Enable removal of points with weigth zero.

parameters.name: Line Strip Behavior
parameters.label: Line Strip Behavior
parameters.description: What to do when points of a line strip are deleted.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Sets the overall transform order for the transformations.

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: Sets the order of the rotations within the overall transform order.

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description: Translate the field in the three axes.

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description: Rotate the points around the corresponding X, Y and Z axes. Angles are given in degrees.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description: These three fields scale the Source geometry in the three axes.

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description: The pivot point for the transform rotates and scales.

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.label: Weight
parameters.description: Output a field weight attribute.

parameters.name: Signed Distance
parameters.label: Signed Distance
parameters.description: Output a signed distance attribute for each point.

parameters.name: Per-Field Weights
parameters.label: Per-Field Weights
parameters.description: Output an attribute array value for each field in the Specification POP.

parameters.name: Per-Field Distances
parameters.label: Per-Field Distances
parameters.description: Output an attribute array value for each field in the Specification POP.

parameters.name: Combine Operation
parameters.label: Combine Operation
parameters.description: Specify how to combine the output value with the combine attribute value.

parameters.name: Combine Entity
parameters.label: Combine Entity
parameters.description: Specify which computed value to use for the combine operation.

parameters.name: Combine Attribute
parameters.label: Combine Attribute
parameters.description: Input attribute scope for the combine operation.

parameters.name: Combine Output Attribute
parameters.label: Combine Output Attribute
parameters.description: Output attribute scope for the combine operation.

parameters.name: Override Automatic Attribute
parameters.label: Override Automatic Attribute
parameters.description: Whether to override the kind of attribute automatically created based on the POP input and parameters. Allows to specify manually the type and number of components of the new attribute.

parameters.name: Attribute Type
parameters.label: Attribute Type
parameters.description: The combined output attribute's data type, default float.

parameters.name: Components
parameters.label: Components
parameters.description: The number of components in the new custom attribute.

parameters.name: Default Value
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: Default values of the output attribute components if they cannot be computed.
